verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is primarily a type I single-pass ER transmembrane protein (N-terminus in ER lumen, C-terminus cytosolic) functioning as an ER Ca²⁺ sensor for SOCE. However, a documented minority plasma membrane pool (STIM1PM) exists where STIM1 is stably inserted in the plasma membrane with an extracellular N-terminal domain; this has been confirmed by flow cytometry and ELISA on intact cells, and N-glycosylation facilitates its PM trafficking and stability. The ER-PM junction accumulation during SOCE activation keeps STIM1 in the ER membrane (not the PM). The genuine STIM1PM pool — a real TM insertion at the outer leaflet — qualifies as dual_localization: dominant ER residence with a documented, stably anchored surface minority pool.
